This documentation is for an out-of-date version of Apache Flink. We recommend you use [the latest stable version](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-stable/dev/python/installation.html>).

[ ](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.12/zh/dev/python/installation.html>)

# Installation

**This documentation is for an out-of-date version of Apache Flink. We recommend you use[the latest stable version](<https://nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-stable/dev/python/installation.html>).**

  * Environment Requirements
  * Environment Setup
  * Installation of PyFlink

## Environment Requirements

Note Python version (3.5, 3.6, 3.7 or 3.8) is required for PyFlink. Please run the following command to make sure that it meets the requirements:


    $ python --version
    # the version printed here must be 3.5, 3.6, 3.7 or 3.8

## Environment Setup

Your system may include multiple Python versions, and thus also include multiple Python binary executables. You can run the following `ls` command to find out what Python binary executables are available in your system:


    $ ls /usr/bin/python*

To satisfy the PyFlink requirement regarding the Python environment version, you can choose to soft link `python` to point to your `python3` interpreter:


    ln -s /usr/bin/python3 python

In addition to creating a soft link, you can also choose to create a Python virtual environment (`venv`). You can refer to the [Preparing Python Virtual Environment](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.12/dev/python/faq.html#preparing-python-virtual-environment>) documentation page for details on how to achieve that setup.

If you don’t want to use a soft link to change the system’s `python` interpreter point to, you can use the configuration way to specify the Python interpreter. For specifying the Python interpreter used to compile the jobs, you can refer to the [python client executable](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.12/dev/python/python_config.html#python-client-executable>) For specifying the Python interpreter used to execute the python UDF worker, you can refer to the [python.executable](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.12/dev/python/python_config.html#python-executable>)

## Installation of PyFlink

PyFlink is available through [PyPi](<https://pypi.org/project/apache-flink/>) as a regular Python package and can be installed as follows:


    $ python -m pip install apache-flink 1.12.7

You can also build PyFlink from source by following the [development guide](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.12/flinkDev/building.html#build-pyflink>).

Note Starting from Flink 1.11, it’s also supported to run PyFlink jobs locally on Windows and so you could develop and debug PyFlink jobs on Windows.

[ Want to contribute translation? ](<https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/display/FLINK/Flink+Translation+Specifications>)
